test_cases.input: Podcast interview with a climate scientist at a major research institution. Topic: Recent findings on methane emissions from permafrost thaw and their implications for climate projections. Format: podcast, 20 minutes, lightly edited. Tone: Collaborative. Outcomes: clear explanation for general audience, one surprising finding, personal stake in the work.
test_cases.output_summary: Produced 4 thematic blocks moving from background to research findings to implications to personal dimension. Questions in spoken register throughout. [MUST-ASK] tags on core findings and implications questions. Closing question invited unrehearsed personal reflection. All 18 questions open-ended. Sensitive uncertainty questions had alternative phrasings. Pacing appropriate for 20-minute podcast.

test_cases.input: TV interview with a local city councillor responsible for housing policy. Topic: New housing strategy critics say will displace low-income renters. Format: TV news, 10 minutes, will be edited. Tone: Adversarial — councillor under scrutiny. Outcomes: response to displacement criticism, timeline accountability, acknowledgement of trade-offs.
test_cases.output_summary: Adversarial framing correctly reflected across 3 blocks (context, displacement challenge, accountability). [MUST-ASK] on displacement response and timeline. Alternative phrasings provided for direct challenges. Questions are short and TV-ready. Closing invites acknowledgement of trade-offs. Minor: 10-minute constraint not explicitly addressed in sequencing guidance.

test_cases.input: Magazine profile interview with a documentary filmmaker. Topic: New film on stateless people living in legal limbo, and her working process. Format: print/magazine, 30 minutes, collaborative. Outcomes: personal origin story for the film, craft and process, relationship with subjects, future direction.
test_cases.output_summary: 5 thematic blocks covering origins, subjects, craft, film itself, future. Questions longer and more discursive — appropriate for magazine register. [MUST-ASK] on why this subject, what happened to filmed subjects, hardest moment, next project. Closing maximally open-ended. 22 questions total. Minor: craft block slightly overweighted relative to human story dimension.
